perItem.jp: 本当のことを言うべきだ
知らぬ人に声をかけられた
今日は来なくてもいいです
今日は休むわけにはいきません
窓を開けてもいいですか。
先生がおっしゃいました。

proposals.title: Add honorific irregular-aru verbs (おっしゃる/なさる/いらっしゃる/くださる/ござる) to the conjugation maps
proposals.rationale: The rubric's named D1 failure: おっしゃる is a godan honorific verb whose 連用形 softens irregularly to おっしゃい (not おっしゃり), so its ます形 is おっしゃいます. The library's IrregularConjugationMap is a closed set covering only する/来る/くる, and GodanVerb mechanically yields おっしゃります. With no honest way to model the verb, the annotate output (r6-a2) faked it as IchidanVerb{stem:'おっしゃい';ending:'る'} purely to force MasuPast=おっしゃいました — which simultaneously implies a bogus Dictionary form おっしゃいる. This same gap drives 6 of the 7 r6-a2 issues (D1/D4/D6) and recurs for the whole -aru honorific family taught in i04.ts/a01.ts.
proposals.change: In <repo>/src/verb-types.d.ts add a HonorificAruVerb class (e.g. { type: 'honorific-aru'; dictionary: 'おっしゃる'|'なさる'|'いらっしゃる'|'くださる'|'ござる' }) plus a map giving each its irregular 連用形 (おっしゃい, なさい, いらっしゃい, ください, ござい) and dictionary/Te/Ta/Imperative forms, so GetMasuStem yields the い-stem and Masu/MasuPast resolve to おっしゃいます/おっしゃいました. Wire it into Verb and ConjugateVerb. Then update annotate's API_REFERENCE to list these dictionary keys.

proposals.title: Forbid faking verb class to force a surface; never reclassify a godan verb as ichidan
proposals.rationale: Independent of whether the -aru class is added, the annotate output committed a wrong-class error (modeling honorific godan おっしゃる as IchidanVerb so the surface coincidentally resolved). The rubric explicitly names this as a 0 on D1 even when the surface type-checks. The prompt has no rule against picking a verb class for its conjugation side-effect, and no documented honest fallback (a01.ts uses a raw literal おっしゃいました with an explanatory comment). A single hard rule prevents this whole category for any verb the conjugator cannot model.
proposals.change: In annotate.ts Hard rules add: 'Choose a verb's class by its real conjugation type (godan/ichidan/irregular), NEVER by which class happens to make the target surface resolve. Reclassifying a godan verb as IchidanVerb (or vice versa) to force a form is a hard error even if it type-checks — verify the implied Dictionary form is a real word. If the library cannot produce a verb's correct surface (e.g. honorific -aru verbs whose 連用形 is irregular), declare the verb in its true class and write the unmodelable surface as a documented raw literal rather than faking the class.'

proposals.title: Decompose frozen predicate tails in chapter snippets (かけられた / なくてもいいです / わけにはいきません)
proposals.rationale: Three chapter snippets (r6-c2 a03, r6-c3 e08, r6-c4 i10) each dump a multi-morpheme predicate as one raw literal, hiding modellable verbs, particles, and conjugations — the single largest cluster of high-severity D2/D3/D4 issues (≈30 of 60). Each is fully expressible today: かけられた = VerbPart<かける(ichidan),'Passive'> + た literal; わけにはいきません = CommonNoun<'わけ'> + に + は + VerbPart<行く,'Masen'>; いいです = AdjectivePart<いい,'Polite'> with the も/て particles exposed. The conjugator already supports Masen, Passive, and polite adjective forms, so these are authoring slips, not library gaps.
proposals.change: Edit a03.ts (知らぬ人に声をかけられた), e08.ts (今日は来なくてもいいです), and i10.ts (今日は休むわけにはいきません): rebuild each frozen tail as a Sentence/typed-part composition — declare かける as IchidanVerb and use ConjugateVerb Passive; declare 行く as GodanVerb and use Masen; expose わけ as CommonNoun and に/は/も/て as their own ParticlePart units; render いい via the irregular IAdjective Polite form. Keep only genuinely unmodelable bits (e.g. classical ぬ) as documented literals.

proposals.title: Split べきだ in 本当のことを言うべきだ into べき (literal) + Copula<"Plain">, and expose の
proposals.rationale: r6-c0 (a12) accounts for ~12 issues: the genitive の is buried in a template literal `${本当}の${こと}` (D3 high), and べきだ collapses the modellable copula だ with the unmodelable formal-noun べき (D2/D4/D6). The copula is expressible via Copula<'Plain'>=だ and の must be its own ParticlePart; only べき need remain a literal. This is a one-off authoring fix, not a library gap.
proposals.change: Edit a12.ts: replace the `${本当}の${こと}` glue with CommonNoun<'本当'> + ParticlePart<'の'> + CommonNoun<'こと'>, keep を as a ParticlePart, model 言う as VerbPart, and split the tail into SuffixPart<'べき'> + CopulaPart<'Plain'> instead of the raw literal べきだ.

proposals.title: Add a negative-auxiliary (ない) adjectival conjugation so なくて / なく is modellable
proposals.rationale: Multiple r6-c3 issues (D6 high, D4/D6 ts-def) note that verb 'Nai' returns only the 未然形 stem (来/こ), forcing authors to bolt なくて on as a literal that splits one negative morpheme across a tag and a string. ない inflects like an i-adjective (なく/なくて/なかった), so a NegativeAuxiliary form would let なくてもいい and similar permission/obligation constructions be built honestly across many future sentences rather than frozen.
proposals.change: In <repo>/src extend the conjugator so a verb's negative can yield i-adjective-like forms of ない: e.g. a 'NaiAdverbial' (来なく) and 'NaiTe' (来なくて), or expose ない as a conjugatable auxiliary i-adjective that mounts on the Nai-stem. Then document it in annotate's API_REFERENCE and reference it in the なくて hard rule.

issues.note: The genitive/linking particle の is embedded inside the literal `${本当}の${こと}` instead of being expressed as its own visible particle unit; only を is properly extracted via PhraseWithParticle.
issues.jp: 本当のことを言うべきだ

issues.note: 本当のこと is partially fused: の is hidden in a template literal rather than decomposed into noun + particle + noun.
issues.jp: 本当のことを言うべきだ

issues.note: べきだ is a raw literal because the library has no Beki/auxiliary construct for べき + copula; honest but not modellable.
issues.jp: 本当のことを言うべきだ

issues.note: べき is a 形式名詞 (formal/auxiliary noun) the library has no constructor for, so it can only appear as a literal; POS for べき is therefore lost.
issues.jp: 本当のことを言うべきだ

issues.note: The copula だ is expressible via Copula<"Plain"> (= だ) but is hardcoded into the べきだ literal instead, an avoidable raw literal for a modellable inflection.
issues.jp: 本当のことを言うべきだ

issues.note: べきだ collapses two morphemes (べき + だ) into one literal chunk; at minimum だ could be split out via Copula, leaving べき as the only honest literal.
issues.jp: 本当のことを言うべきだ

issues.note: The attributive particle の is glued as a raw literal in `${本当}の${こと}` instead of being exposed as a separate particle; particle integrity is broken.
issues.jp: 本当のことを言うべきだ

issues.note: べきだ is a single raw literal. The copula だ should be Copula<"Plain"> (the library provides it); べき is an unmodeled bound auxiliary that should be isolated, not fused with だ.
issues.jp: 本当のことを言うべきだ

issues.note: Sentence-final だ uses a raw literal instead of the available Copula/ConjugateCopula constructor.
issues.jp: 本当のことを言うべきだ

issues.note: べきだ collapses two morphemes (auxiliary べき + copula だ) into one chunk, hiding internal structure.
issues.jp: 本当のことを言うべきだ

issues.note: 寒い is declared as `IAdjective & { stem: "寒"; ending: "い" }` instead of the canonical `{ type: "i-adjective"; stem: "寒"; ending: "い" }`. It still type-checks and classifies as an i-adjective via the intersection, but the non-standard authoring style is slightly less clear than other chapters.
issues.jp: 昨日は寒かった

issues.note: かけられた is a raw string hiding verb かける + passive られ + past た; should decompose using ConjugateVerb<かける,'Passive'>='かけられ' then append た, since かける is fully modellable.
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: The sentence's main verb かける is never declared as an IchidanVerb; its POS is entirely lost inside the literal.
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: 知らぬ uses ConjugateVerb<知る,'Nai'> for 知ら then appends literal ぬ; the library has no classical negative-attributive ぬ form, so the literal is a defensible workaround.
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: No combined Passive+Past form exists, so た must be a literal even if かけられ were conjugated; minor library gap.
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: かけられた hides the main verb かける (ichidan) plus passive られ plus past た in one literal; should be built from a verb constructor + ConjugateVerb Passive form.
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: Passive form is modellable: type かける = IchidanVerb & {stem:'かけ';ending:'る'}; ConjugateVerb<かける,'Passive'> yields かけられ, then a た literal — far more honest than the raw literal かけられた.
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: ぬ (classical negative attributive) has no library form, so Nai-stem 知ら + literal ぬ is an acceptable approximation, but the 'Nai' form name slightly misrepresents the ぬ auxiliary.
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: The main verb かける is absent as a constructor; the predicate of the sentence carries no POS.
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: かけられた is the sentence's main predicate (ichidan かける + Passive られ + past た). The library can produce ConjugateVerb<かける,'Passive'> = かけられ; appending た would be honest. Leaving it a raw literal with no かける verb declared hides the core grammar.
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: The predicate verb かける is not modeled in any constructor (no GodanVerb/IchidanVerb declaration), so the only verb actually typed is the relative-clause 知る.
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: かけられた is a single leaf hiding verb stem + passive auxiliary + past auxiliary.
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: ぬ (classical negative auxiliary) has no dedicated form; using Nai (未然形) + literal ぬ is a defensible workaround given the library lacks a 文語 negative form.
issues.jp: 知らぬ人に声をかけられた

issues.note: なくてもいいです is a single raw literal hiding negative auxiliary なく, conjunctive て, particle も, i-adjective いい, and copula です. Only 来 comes from a constructor.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: The particle も is buried inside the literal string and never appears as its own unit.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: Permission construction frozen wholesale; いい/です are modellable via ConjugateAdjective<いい,"Polite"> and the negative via the conjugator, so the literal needlessly discards structure.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: です polite copula should derive from the conjugator (library supports ConjugateAdjective Polite = いいです) rather than being a raw literal.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: い-adjective いい and copula です are absent from any POS constructor, hidden in the literal tail.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: なくてもいいです is one collapsed literal; at minimum です (Copula<'Polite'>) and いい (ConjugateAdjective<いい,'Basic'>) should be separate composed units, exposing the negative adverbial なく + て + も structure.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: Particle も (and te-linker て) hidden inside the literal string instead of being visible morphemes.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: Polite です written as a raw literal though the library provides Copula<'Polite'>=です.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: い-adjective いい hidden in the literal rather than modeled as an IAdjective.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: The negative auxiliary ない's adverbial なく has no dedicated constructor, so the なくて span is hard to express fully even with best effort; only the literal-everything choice is the authoring slip.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: なくてもいいです collapses ない/なくて/も/いい/です into a single literal; at minimum いいです should be ConjugateAdjective<いい,'Polite'> and the も particle made visible.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: Concessive particle も is hidden inside the literal; it should be its own visible unit.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: The negative auxiliary ない and its te-form なくて cannot be generated by the conjugator (verb 'Nai' returns only the 未然形 stem 来/こ), so the author bolts なくて onto the stem as a literal, splitting one negative morpheme across a tag and a string — misleading. Library lacks auxiliary-adjective (ない) conjugation and a なくてもいい permission construction.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: いいです is expressible via the i-adjective Polite form (special いい handling exists) but was frozen as a raw literal.
issues.jp: 今日は来なくてもいいです

issues.note: わけにはいきません dumped as one raw literal; should be CommonNoun<"わけ"> + particle に + particle は + ConjugateVerb<行く,"Masen">.
issues.jp: 今日は休むわけにはいきません

issues.note: Particles に and は are hidden inside the literal には instead of being visible PhraseWithParticle units.
issues.jp: 今日は休むわけにはいきません

issues.note: いきません is 行く + Masen; library supports Masen form (line 32) so it should not be a literal.
issues.jp: 今日は休むわけにはいきません

issues.note: Verb 行く and formal noun わけ receive no constructor / POS classification at all.
issues.jp: 今日は休むわけにはいきません

issues.note: わけにはいきません is one frozen literal; should decompose into わけ (formal/CommonNoun), particle に, particle は, and 行く conjugated.
issues.jp: 今日は休むわけにはいきません

issues.note: Particles に and は are buried inside the literal instead of being their own units (e.g. via PhraseWithParticle).
issues.jp: 今日は休むわけにはいきません

issues.note: いきません should be ConjugateVerb<行く,"Masen"> (行く is a GodanVerb the library can conjugate to ません); using a raw literal misses the modellable negative-polite form.
issues.jp: 今日は休むわけにはいきません

issues.note: 行く and わけ get no constructor at all because the tail is a literal; the verb's godan class and the formal noun are lost.
issues.jp: 今日は休むわけにはいきません

issues.note: わけにはいきません is dumped as one raw literal, hiding the formal noun わけ, particles に/は, and the verb いく. Should decompose to CommonNoun<"わけ"> + に + は + ConjugateVerb<いく,"Masen">.
issues.jp: 今日は休むわけにはいきません

issues.note: Particles に and は are buried inside the literal わけにはいきません instead of being their own visible units.
issues.jp: 今日は休むわけにはいきません

issues.note: いきません (行きません) is a polite negative of godan いく that the library can produce via ConjugateVerb<いく,"Masen"> (Masen form exists), but it is frozen as a literal.
issues.jp: 今日は休むわけにはいきません

issues.note: わけ (形式名詞) and いく are content words with no constructor; they live only in the raw literal.
issues.jp: 今日は休むわけにはいきません

issues.note: The whole わけにはいかない idiomatic predicate plus a modellable conjugation is hidden in one literal, contrary to how the sentence would be taught.
issues.jp: 今日は休むわけにはいきません

issues.note: おっしゃる modeled as IchidanVerb; it is a godan honorific verb. The author froze the irregular ます-stem おっしゃい into stem and used ending る so MasuPast (stem+ました) coincidentally surfaces correctly. Wrong verb class — the rubric's named example.
issues.jp: 先生がおっしゃいました。

issues.note: The ichidan misclassification is internally inconsistent: ConjugateVerb<this,"Dictionary"> would give おっしゃいる, a non-word. Misleading grammatical analysis.
issues.jp: 先生がおっしゃいました。

issues.note: The library's IrregularConjugationMap only covers する/来る/くる, so honorific godan verbs with irregular ます-stems (おっしゃる, いらっしゃる, なさる) cannot be faithfully conjugated. The honest path the library uses elsewhere (a01.ts, i04.ts) is a raw template literal おっしゃいました; the ts-def gap is what pushed the author to fake an ichidan verb.
issues.jp: 先生がおっしゃいました。

issues.note: 先生 wrapped in POS-agnostic NounPart, hiding that it is a common noun (普通名詞) rather than using a common-noun-specific part.
issues.jp: 先生がおっしゃいました。

issues.note: おっしゃる declared as IchidanVerb; it is an honorific godan (う-verb). The ichidan model with stem 'おっしゃい' is chosen purely to make MasuPast resolve to おっしゃいました, misrepresenting the part of speech / verb class. The codebase's own a01.ts comment confirms it is godan.
issues.jp: 先生がおっしゃいました。

issues.note: Segmentation does not match how the verb is analyzed grammatically. Honest options: model as GodanVerb and accept the library cannot derive the irregular おっしゃい ます形 (spell おっしゃいました as a documented literal, as the reference does), rather than forcing an ichidan reclassification.
issues.jp: 先生がおっしゃいました。

issues.note: The library's GodanVerb conjugation produces the regular おっしゃり for MasuStem and has no way to express the irregular おっしゃい ます形 of honorific godan verbs (おっしゃる/なさる/いらっしゃる/くださる), which is the root pressure that led the author to misclass the verb.
issues.jp: 先生がおっしゃいました。

issues.note: おっしゃる is a special honorific godan (-aru) verb whose 連用形 is irregular (おっしゃい, not おっしゃり). It is misfiled as IchidanVerb with stem 'おっしゃい'/ending 'る'. The library has no honorific/irregular-aru verb class, so the author abused IchidanVerb to force the surface. A proper GodanVerb { stem:'おっしゃ'; ending:'る' } would wrongly yield おっしゃります, hence the workaround.
issues.jp: 先生がおっしゃいました。

issues.note: MasuPast surface おっしゃいました is correct, but only because the ichidan stem was hand-set to おっしゃい; the conjugator is not genuinely modeling the irregular 連用形. The same fake-ichidan model implies a bogus dictionary form おっしゃいる.
issues.jp: 先生がおっしゃいました。

issues.note: Inflection is technically via ConjugateVerb but rests on a wrong verb class; honest modeling of this honorific verb is impossible with the current types.
issues.jp: 先生がおっしゃいました。
